stem: In triangle $PQR$, angle $P = 47°$ and angle $Q = 68°$. What is the measure of angle $R$?
explanation:
The sum of the interior angles of any triangle equals $180°$. Given angle $P = 47°$ and angle $Q = 68°$, we find angle $R$ by subtracting:

$$\angle R = 180° - 47° - 68° = 180° - 115° = 65°$$

The measure of angle $R$ is $65°$.

distractorRationale.A: The student subtracts only one of the given angles from $180°$ — specifically $180° - 47° - 78°$ by misreading $68°$ as $78°$ — arriving at $55°$.
distractorRationale.C: The student adds the two given angles ($47° + 68° = 115°$) but then subtracts from $190°$ instead of $180°$, yielding $75°$.
distractorRationale.D: The student computes only the sum of the two given angles ($47° + 68° = 115°$) and reports that sum as the answer, forgetting to subtract from $180°$.
